description:
Partial benchmark — full buck2 build through FSKit still blocked on a separate symlink-op issue (ENXIO from buck2's materializer when issuing relative-target symlink syscalls into the mount), but base FSKit traversal numbers are now in.

Setup: macFUSE 5.2.0 FSKit on macOS 26.4.1 Apple Silicon. Mount at /tmp/tk-bench3, repo at /Users/yann/src/github.com/firefly-engineering/turnkey, output buck-out -> /tmp/tk-bench3-out.

Native baseline cold build of //src/rust/composition:composition: 5.9s (83 commands all local).

FSKit traversal microbenchmarks (cache warm):

  Workload                                     Native   FSKit    Overhead
  find <repo>/src -type f          (282 files)   5 ms   132 ms     ~26x
  cat <repo>/src/rust/composition/**.rs        11 ms    47 ms      ~4.3x
                                   (518 KB / 16 files)

Important caveat: the daemon-side FUSE metrics for these runs show only statfs:1 — most ops bypassed our callbacks because /root cells and toolchains are exposed as symlinks (per the recent 2b5af2f commit 'output mounts as symlinks instead of FUSE pass-through'). The numbers above are the user-observed cost of accessing things THROUGH the FSKit mount (kernel-side symlink resolution + FSKit volume-id lookups), not pure FUSE callback round-trip overhead. Real FUSE overhead happens for files served virtually (.buckconfig, .envrc, .buckroot) and for the writable buck-out output mount.

To complete the original 'buck2 build root//... vs native ~1min' comparison, we still need to:

  * Resolve the symlink-op ENXIO. Buck2's materializer issues
    symlink(target='../../../../../../external/rustdeps/.../README.md',
            link='/private/<mount>/buck-out/.turnkey/art/...')
    and gets 'Device not configured (os error 6)'. Likely a macFUSE FSKit
    bug or quirk around relative symlink targets across cell boundaries.
    Worth a small Rust unit test + minimal repro to file upstream.
  * Once symlink works, run a full cold build through FSKit and capture
    per-callback FUSE metrics for the comparison. A reasonable target
    would be //src/rust/composition or //src/cmd/turnkey-composed.

Conclusion against the original 'under 2x of native' success criterion:
unverifiable until buck2 can complete a build through FSKit. Initial
traversal numbers (4-26x for read-only ops) suggest 2x is aspirational,
but remember most of that overhead is kernel + FSKit volume layer, not
FUSE per se. The 8-9x FUSE-T NFS slowdown the parent epic cites would
need re-measuring on Tahoe with current FUSE-T 1.2.x for a fair compare.
title: Benchmark macFUSE FSKit vs FUSE-T NFS performance
